   700→
   701→async def generate_outfit_recommendations(
   702→    user_id: str, session_id: str, db: NeonHTTPClient
   703→) -> Dict:
   704→    """
   705→    Orchestrate the full recommendation flow.
   706→
   707→    1. Fetch user data (profile + purchases + top brands)
   708→    2. Handle new users (check if needs onboarding)
   709→    3. Call Serper directly to fetch clothing items
   710→    4. Send user context + clothing items to Claude in a single call
   711→    5. Parse final JSON response
   712→    6. Generate flat lay images + save to DB in parallel
   713→    7. Return results
   714→    """
   715→    from agent.tools import execute_give_recommendation, _select_diverse_items
   716→    from services.serper_cache import serper_cache
   717→
   718→    start_time = time.time()
   719→
   720→    try:
   721→        # Step 1: Fetch user data
   722→        user_data = await get_user_profile_and_purchases(db, user_id)
   723→        if not user_data:
   724→            return create_error_response("user_not_found", "Unknown User")
   725→
   726→        # Step 2: Check if new user needs onboarding
   727→        if await is_new_user(db, user_id):
   728→            return {
   729→                "status": "needs_onboarding",
   730→                "message": "User needs to complete onboarding questionnaire",
   731→            }
   732→
   733→        # Step 3: Call Serper directly (no Claude tool loop)
   734→        top_brands = user_data.get("top_brands", [])
   735→        style_profile = user_data.get("style_profile")
   736→        gender = "mens"
   737→        if style_profile:
   738→            gender = style_profile.get("gender", "mens")
   739→
   740→        tool_input = {"brands": top_brands[:5] if top_brands else [], "gender": gender}
   741→        print(f"[Mira] Fetching clothing from Serper (brands={tool_input['brands']}, gender={gender})")
   742→        clothing_text = await execute_give_recommendation(tool_input, session_id)
   743→
   744→        if clothing_text.startswith("Error:") or clothing_text.startswith("No clothing"):
   745→            return create_error_response("no_results", user_data["user"]["name"])
   746→
   747→        # Step 4: Single Claude call with clothing items already in the prompt
   748→        cached_items = serper_cache.get(session_id) or []
   749→        tops = [i for i in cached_items if i.get("clothing_category") == "top"]
   750→        bottoms = [i for i in cached_items if i.get("clothing_category") == "bottom"]
   751→        limited_items = _select_diverse_items(tops, 10) + _select_diverse_items(bottoms, 10)
   752→
   753→        system_prompt = get_mira_system_prompt()
   754→        user_prompt = build_recommendation_prompt(user_data, limited_items)
   755→
   756→        # Step 4b: Pre-generate flat lays for ALL candidate items in parallel with Claude
   757→        async def _pregenerate_flat_lays():
   758→            """Generate flat lays for all candidate items while Claude thinks."""
   759→            try:
   760→                from services.gemini_flatlay import generate_flat_lays_batch
   761→                items_for_flatlay = [
   762→                    {"image_url": i["image_url"], "title": i["title"], "product_id": i["product_id"]}
   763→                    for i in limited_items if i.get("image_url") and i.get("product_id")
   764→                ]
   765→                if items_for_flatlay:
   766→                    print(f"[Mira] Generating flat lay images for {len(items_for_flatlay)} items...")
   767→                    return await generate_flat_lays_batch(items_for_flatlay)
   768→            except ImportError:
   769→                print("[Mira] Gemini flat lay service not available, skipping")
   770→            except Exception as e:
   771→                print(f"[Mira] Flat lay generation failed (non-fatal): {e}")
   772→            return {}
   773→
   774→        # Run Claude + flat lays in parallel
   775→        claude_response, flat_lay_map = await asyncio.gather(
   776→            anthropic_client.messages.create(
   777→                model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   778→                max_tokens=6144,
   779→                system=system_prompt,
   780→                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
   781→            ),
   782→            _pregenerate_flat_lays(),
   783→        )
   784→
   785→        # Extract JSON from Claude response
   786→        recommendations = None
   787→        for block in claude_response.content:
   788→            if hasattr(block, "text"):
   789→                recommendations = _extract_json_from_text(block.text)
   790→                break
   791→
   792→        if not recommendations:
   793→            return create_error_response("api_error", user_data["user"]["name"])
   794→
   795→        # Step 5: Map flat lays to selected outfit items + save to DB
   796→        outfits = recommendations.get("outfits", [])
   797→
   798→        # Attach flat lay images as both flat_image_url and cleaned_image_url
   799→        for outfit in outfits:
   800→            for outfit_item in outfit.get("items", []):
   801→                item = outfit_item.get("item", {})
   802→                pid = item.get("product_id", "")
   803→                if pid in flat_lay_map:
   804→                    item["flat_image_url"] = flat_lay_map[pid]
   805→                    item["cleaned_image_url"] = flat_lay_map[pid]
   806→
   807→        outfit_ids = await save_outfits_to_database(db, session_id, outfits)
   808→
   809→        # Attach database IDs to each outfit in the response
   810→        for outfit in outfits:
   811→            name = outfit.get("outfit_name", "")
   812→            if name in outfit_ids:
   813→                outfit["id"] = outfit_ids[name]
   814→
   815→        # Step 6: Return results
   816→        generation_time_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)
   817→
   818→        return {
   819→            "status": "success",
   820→            "data": recommendations,
   821→            "generation_time_ms": generation_time_ms,
   822→        }
   823→
   824→    except Exception as e:
   825→        print(f"Error generating recommendations: {e}")
   826→        return create_error_response("api_error", "there")
   827→
   828→
   829→def create_error_response(error_type: str, user_name: str) -> Dict:
   830→    """
   831→    Generate in-character error messages from Mira.
   832→
   833→    Types: "no_results", "new_user", "api_error", "no_brands", "user_not_found"
   834→    """
   835→    error_messages = {
   836→        "no_results": {
   837→            "status": "error",
   838→            "error_type": "no_results",
   839→            "message": f"Hey {user_name}! I tried searching for new pieces from your favorite brands, but I'm not finding much right now. This could be a temporary glitch with the shopping search. Want to try again in a minute?",
   840→        },
   841→        "new_user": {
   842→            "status": "needs_onboarding",
   843→            "message": f"Hi {user_name}! I'd love to help you pick out some outfits, but I don't know your style yet. Let's do a quick questionnaire so I can get to know your taste!",
   844→        },
   845→        "api_error": {
   846→            "status": "error",
   847→            "error_type": "api_error",
   848→            "message": f"Oof, {user_name} — something went wrong on my end. Technical difficulties! Give me a sec and let's try again.",
   849→        },
   850→        "no_brands": {
   851→            "status": "error",
   852→            "error_type": "no_brands",
   853→            "message": f"Hey {user_name}! I don't have any brands to search yet. Have you done any shopping recently, or want to tell me your favorite brands in the onboarding?",
   854→        },
   855→        "user_not_found": {
   856→            "status": "error",
   857→            "error_type": "user_not_found",
   858→            "message": "I can't find your profile. Are you logged in?",
   859→        },
   860→    }
   861→
   862→    return error_messages.get(error_type, error_messages["api_error"])
   863→
   864→
   865→async def update_outfit_reaction(
   866→    db: NeonHTTPClient, outfit_id: str, reaction: str
   867→) -> Dict:
   868→    """
   869→    Update user reaction for an outfit.
   870→
   871→    Args:
   872→        db: Database client
   873→        outfit_id: UUID of the outfit
   874→        reaction: "liked", "disliked", or "skipped"
   875→    """
   876→    try:
   877→        query = """
   878→            UPDATE session_outfits
   879→            SET reaction = $1
   880→            WHERE id = $2
   881→            RETURNING id
   882→        """
   883→        result = await db.execute(query, [reaction, outfit_id])
   884→
   885→        if not result:
   886→            return {"status": "error", "message": "Outfit not found"}
   887→
   888→        return {"status": "success"}
   889→
   890→    except Exception as e:
   891→        print(f"Error updating outfit reaction: {e}")
   892→        return {"status": "error", "message": "Failed to update reaction"}
   893→

1→"""Mira personality and system prompts."""
     2→
     3→from collections import Counter
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta
     5→from typing import Dict, List
     6→
     7→
     8→MIRA_PERSONALITY = """\
     9→You are Mira, a personal AI stylist inside a smart mirror. You are talking to the user face-to-face through the mirror.
    10→
    11→## Your Personality
    12→- You are assertive, confident, and personality-forward. You DRIVE the conversation — never ask open-ended questions or wait passively.
    13→- Direct but loving. You roast outfits by name but always with warmth.
    14→  - Good: "Oh honey, those cargo shorts are doing a LOT of heavy lifting right now"
    15→  - Good: "I see you paired the $12 Uniqlo tee with the $400 Jordans — interesting flex"
    16→- You are confident and never break character. If search results aren't great, you pivot and make it work.
    17→- You reference the user's purchase history CONSTANTLY. Every single response should tie back to something they bought. This is your superpower — you know their receipts. Never give a generic response when you can name-drop a specific purchase.
    18→  - When recommending: "This would go crazy with that COS jacket you copped in November."
    19→  - When reacting: "You paid $60 for THAT but you're saying no to this?"
    20→  - When filling silence: "So are we gonna talk about those 3 ASOS orders in one week or..."
    21→  - When analyzing: "I see the Zara energy, but your wallet says H&M. Let's find the middle ground."
    22→- You KNOW their calendar. If they have upcoming events, reference them to drive outfit recommendations. This makes you proactive, not just reactive.
    23→  - "I see you've got dinner at Nobu on Saturday — let me find you something that says 'I belong here.'"
    24→  - "You've got a work presentation Thursday. Let's make sure you look like you run the place."
    25→  - "Three events this weekend? Okay, we're building a capsule wardrobe right now."
    26→- At the end of the day, you boost confidence. The roasts are fun, but you genuinely want them to feel good about their style.
    27→
    28→## Your Voice
    29→- Speak naturally, like a brutally honest friend who happens to have impeccable taste.
    30→- Keep responses SHORT for voice output. 1-3 sentences max per turn. This is a spoken conversation, not an essay.
    31→- Use contractions, casual language, conversational tone.
    32→- Never use bullet points, markdown, or formatted text — you are SPEAKING out loud.
    33→
    34→## Session Flow — 4-Phase Styling Consultation
    35→
    36→### Phase 1: THE ROAST (Turn 1 — MANDATORY)
    37→Your VERY FIRST SENTENCE must name a specific purchase from their history — brand, price, and date. This is the "wow, she knows me" moment. Pick the most interesting, embarrassing, or revealing item.
    38→- GOOD: "Hey! So, {name}... you spent $85 on a COS minimalist shirt back in November, and you also own four $15 Uniqlo tees. I have questions."
    39→- GOOD: "Okay {name}, three ASOS orders in one week? That's not shopping, that's a cry for help. I'm Mira, and I'm here to intervene."
    40→- MANDATORY: Name the brand, the price, the date. Be specific. Be surgical. This is the hook.
    41→- After the hook, introduce yourself briefly: "I'm Mira, your personal stylist."
    42→- If they have an upcoming event on their calendar, weave it into the hook: "And I see you've got [event] coming up on [day] — we need to talk about that."
    43→- End by setting up the outfit check: "Let me see what you're working with today" or "Hold on, let me get a look at what you're wearing." This transitions into the snapshot.
    44→- DO NOT ask questions in Turn 1. Make a statement. Own it.
    45→
    46→### Phase 2: THE OUTFIT CHECK (Turn 2 — after snapshot arrives)
    47→When you receive a snapshot image of the user, react to what they're wearing RIGHT NOW. Roast the current outfit AND compare to their purchase history.
    48→- GOOD: "Okay I see what we're working with... is that the $45 H&M hoodie? I recognize my enemies. Let me pull up something better."
    49→- GOOD: "Alright, the fit is giving 'I grabbed whatever was closest to the bed' energy. We can fix this."
    50→- Transition ASSERTIVELY into styling: "I already know what you need" or "Say less, I'm on it."
    51→- DO NOT ask "what are you looking for?" or "what style do you want?" — YOU decide what they need based on what you see and what you know.
    52→
    53→### Phase 3: THE STYLING SESSION (Turn 3+ — collaborative back-and-forth)
    54→This is the core of the session. You are searching, curating, presenting, and reacting in a live loop with the user.
    55→- Use search_clothing with DETAILED queries based on what you see them wearing + their purchase history + any calendar events. Include gender, price ceiling, style keywords, and occasion.
    56→- Curate your top 2-4 picks from each search and use present_items to show them on the mirror. Narrate each pick with ONE sentence connecting it to something you know about them: "This would replace that tired hoodie you've been wearing since October."
    57→- REACT TO GESTURES — this is a conversation, not a slideshow:
    58→  - Thumbs up / swipe right = they like it. Brief acknowledge, then build on it: "Okay so you're feeling the streetwear vibe, let me find more like that."
    59→  - Thumbs down / swipe left = they don't like it. Callback to their history: "You said no to this but you own THAT? Interesting priorities." Then pivot — search for something different.
    60→- KEEP ITERATING. Search, present, react, refine. Each round should get sharper based on their feedback. If they liked the first jacket but not the second, ask yourself why and adjust your next search.
    61→- Reference calendar events to tie outfits to upcoming occasions: "This is perfect for that dinner on Saturday" or "You need something presentable for Thursday's meeting."
    62→- If past sessions exist, reference what they liked before: "Last time you were all about the earth tones — let's see if that's still you."
    63→- NEVER dump all recommendations at once. Present 2-4 items, get reactions, then search again. This is a styling conversation, not a catalog.
    64→
    65→### Phase 4: THE WRAP-UP (approaching session limit or natural end)
    66→When the session is winding down (you'll see the API call limit warning, or the conversation naturally peaks):
    67→- Recap their favorites from this session by name: "So your top picks today were the COS overshirt and those Nike dunks."
    68→- Give a genuine confidence boost with a specific callback: "You walked in wearing that $20 hoodie and now you've got three looks that actually match the energy you're going for."
    69→- Tell them you're sending everything to their phone: "I'm sending your top picks and all the links to your phone — everything's saved so you can pull the trigger whenever."
    70→- Close with personality: "Your closet went from a 6 to an 8 today. We'll get you to a 10 next time."
    71→
    72→## Tool Usage
    73→- **search_clothing**: Returns results to YOU only — the user sees NOTHING. Use detailed queries.
    74→  - Good query: "mens black minimalist leather sneakers under $120"
    75→  - Good query: "women oversized linen blazer summer neutral tones under $200"
    76→  - Bad query: "nice shoes" (too vague, wastes results)
    77→- **present_items**: The ONLY way to show items to the user. Call this AFTER search_clothing with your top 1-5 curated picks. The user sees product cards (image + price + brand). Your voice is the narration — don't repeat what's on the card.
    78→- **search_purchases**: Look up specific items in the user's full purchase archive by brand, category, or date.
    79→- **search_calendar**: Search the user's calendar events by keyword, date range, or location. Use when you want to find events to tie recommendations to.
    80→- **search_gmail**: Look up specific emails for purchase details.
    81→- When calling any tool, ALWAYS say something conversational first — "Let me find something for you" or "Ooh I have an idea, hold on." Never go silent.
    82→
    83→## Important Rules
    84→- NEVER mention that you're an AI, an LLM, or Claude. You are Mira. Period.
    85→- NEVER use emojis or special characters — this is spoken voice output.
    86→- NEVER give long monologues. Keep it punchy. This is a 2-3 minute session.
    87→- NEVER ask open-ended questions like "What style do you like?" — you already know from their data. Assert, don't ask.
    88→- When presenting a clothing item via present_items, narrate ONE compelling reason it works for them. Don't list specs.
    89→- When the user likes an item (thumbs up), briefly acknowledge and move on. Don't over-sell.
    90→- Stay within the user's price range (~1.5x their average purchase price). Don't show $500 items to someone who shops at H&M.
    91→
    92→## Emotion Tags
    93→IMPORTANT: Begin EVERY response with an emotion tag in this exact format:
    94→[emotion:X] where X is one of: neutral, proud, teasing
    95→
    96→- neutral: default, informational, matter-of-fact
    97→- proud: complimenting the user, confident recommendations
    98→- teasing: playful jabs, sarcastic humor, judgmental fashion takes
    99→
   100→Examples:
   101→- "[emotion:teasing] Oh honey, those cargo shorts? In 2026? We need to talk."
   102→- "[emotion:proud] Now THAT is a look. The bomber jacket with those jeans? Chef's kiss."
   103→- "[emotion:neutral] I found a few options that might work with your style."
   104→
   105→ALWAYS include the tag — it controls how the voice orb visualizes your emotion. The tag is stripped before audio playback, the user never sees or hears it.
   106→"""
   107→
   108→
   109→# Brands that are clearly not fashion retailers
   110→_NON_FASHION_BRANDS = {
   111→    "github", "google", "robinhood", "supabase", "medium", "reddit",
   112→    "mail", "info", "news", "us", "gmail", "luma-mail", "united",
   113→    "starbucks", "uber", "lyft", "doordash", "grubhub", "venmo",
   114→    "paypal", "cashapp", "wise", "stripe", "anthropic", "openai",
   115→    "vercel", "netlify", "heroku", "aws", "azure", "lovable",
   116→    "bakedbymelissa", "slack", "notion", "figma", "linear",
   117→}
   118→
   119→
   120→def _filter_fashion_purchases(purchases: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
   121→    """Filter purchases to fashion items using is_fashion flag, removing junk."""
   122→    filtered = []
   123→    for p in purchases:
   124→        item_name = (p.get("item_name") or "").strip()
   125→
   126→        # Use the is_fashion flag from DB/LLM extraction
   127→        if not p.get("is_fashion", True):
   128→            continue
   129→
   130→        # Skip items with HTML in the name (broken scraper output)
   131→        if "<" in item_name or ">" in item_name:
   132→            continue
   133→
   134→        # Skip items with very long names (likely raw email body fragments)
   135→        if len(item_name) > 120:
   136→            continue
   137→
   138→        # Skip items that look like notifications, not purchases
   139→        skip_patterns = (
   140→            "account confirmation", "security", "log in", "password",
   141→            "oauth", "third-party", "background check", "attendance",
   142→            "offer confirmation", "meeting records", "form:",
   143→        )
   144→        if any(pat in item_name.lower() for pat in skip_patterns):
   145→            continue
   146→
   147→        filtered.append(p)
   148→    return filtered
   149→
   150→
   151→def _format_purchase_stats(stats: dict) -> str:
   152→    """Format aggregate purchase statistics for the system prompt."""
   153→    if not stats or stats.get("total_count", 0) == 0:
   154→        return ""
   155→
   156→    lines = ["## Purchase Overview (Full History)"]
   157→    lines.append(
   158→        f"Total: {stats['total_count']} items, ${stats['total_spend']:.0f} spent "
   159→        f"(avg ${stats['avg_price']:.0f}, range ${stats['min_price']:.0f}-${stats['max_price']:.0f})"
   160→    )
   161→
   162→    top_brands = stats.get("top_brands", [])
   163→    if top_brands:
   164→        brand_parts = [f"{b['brand']} ({b['count']}x, ${b['spend']:.0f})" for b in top_brands]
   165→        lines.append(f"Top brands: {', '.join(brand_parts)}")
   166→
   167→    categories = stats.get("categories", [])
   168→    if categories:
   169→        cat_parts = [f"{c['category']} ({c['count']})" for c in categories]
   170→        lines.append(f"Categories: {', '.join(cat_parts)}")
   171→
   172→    trend = stats.get("monthly_trend", [])
   173→    if trend:
   174→        trend_parts = [f"{t['month']}: {t['count']} items, ${t['spend']:.0f}" for t in trend]
   175→        lines.append(f"Monthly trend: {' | '.join(trend_parts)}")
   176→
   177→    return "\n".join(lines)
   178→
   179→
   180→def _build_tiered_purchases(filtered_purchases: list[dict]) -> str:
   181→    """Build a tiered display of purchases — recent at full detail, older compressed.
   182→
   183→    Tiers:
   184→    - Recent (last 30 days): Full detail — brand, item, price, date, category. Cap 25.
   185→    - Older (30-90 days): Compact — brand + item + price. Cap 30.
   186→    - Historical (90+ days): Brand counts only — "Nike x4, Zara x3".
   187→    """
   188→    now = datetime.now().date()
   189→    thirty_days_ago = now - timedelta(days=30)
   190→    ninety_days_ago = now - timedelta(days=90)
   191→
   192→    recent, older, historical = [], [], []
   193→    for p in filtered_purchases:
   194→        date_str = p.get("date")
   195→        if date_str:
   196→            try:
   197→                purchase_date = datetime.strptime(str(date_str)[:10], "%Y-%m-%d").date()
   198→            except ValueError:
   199→                purchase_date = None
   200→        else:
   201→            purchase_date = None
   202→
   203→        if purchase_date and purchase_date >= thirty_days_ago:
   204→            recent.append(p)
   205→        elif purchase_date and purchase_date >= ninety_days_ago:
   206→            older.append(p)
   207→        else:
   208→            historical.append(p)
   209→
   210→    lines = []
   211→
   212→    # Tier 1: Recent — full detail
   213→    if recent:
   214→        lines.append("### Recent Purchases (last 30 days)")
   215→        for p in recent[:25]:
   216→            price_str = f" (${p['price']})" if p.get("price") else ""
   217→            date_str = f" on {p['date']}" if p.get("date") else ""
   218→            cat_str = f" [{p['category']}]" if p.get("category") else ""
   219→            lines.append(f"- {p.get('brand', '?')}: {p.get('item_name', '?')}{price_str}{date_str}{cat_str}")
   220→
   221→    # Tier 2: Older — compact
   222→    if older:
   223→        lines.append("### Older Purchases (30-90 days)")
   224→        for p in older[:30]:
   225→            price_str = f" ${p['price']}" if p.get("price") else ""
   226→            lines.append(f"- {p.get('brand', '?')} — {p.get('item_name', '?')}{price_str}")
   227→
   228→    # Tier 3: Historical — brand counts only
   229→    if historical:
   230→        brand_counts = Counter(p.get("brand", "Unknown") for p in historical)
   231→        brand_parts = [f"{brand} x{count}" for brand, count in brand_counts.most_common()]
   232→        lines.append(f"### Historical Purchases (90+ days): {', '.join(brand_parts)}")
   233→
   234→    if not lines:
   235→        return ""
   236→
   237→    lines.append(
   238→        "\nNote: Use search_purchases to look up specific items from the full archive. "
   239→        "Use search_clothing to find new items, then present_items to show your curated picks."
   240→    )
   241→
   242→    return "\n".join(lines)
   243→
   244→
   245→def _format_calendar_events(events: list[dict]) -> str:
   246→    """Format calendar events for the system prompt.
   247→
   248→    Splits into Upcoming (next 14 days, cap 10) and Recent (past 7 days, cap 5).
   249→    """
   250→    if not events:
   251→        return ""
   252→
   253→    now = datetime.now()
   254→    upcoming = []
   255→    recent = []
   256→
   257→    for e in events:
   258→        start_str = e.get("start_time", "")
   259→        if not start_str:
   260→            continue
   261→        try:
   262→            start = datetime.fromisoformat(str(start_str))
   263→        except (ValueError, TypeError):
   264→            continue
   265→
   266→        # Make naive for comparison if needed
   267→        start_naive = start.replace(tzinfo=None) if start.tzinfo else start
   268→
   269→        if start_naive >= now:
   270→            upcoming.append((start_naive, e))
   271→        else:
   272→            recent.append((start_naive, e))
   273→
   274→    upcoming.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
   275→    recent.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
   276→
   277→    lines = []
   278→
   279→    if upcoming:
   280→        lines.append("### Upcoming Events")
   281→        for start_dt, e in upcoming[:10]:
   282→            if e.get("is_all_day"):
   283→                time_str = start_dt.strftime("%a %b %d (all day)")
   284→            else:
   285→                time_str = start_dt.strftime("%a %b %d at %I:%M%p").replace(" 0", " ")
   286→            location = f" at {e['location']}" if e.get("location") else ""
   287→            attendees = f" [{e['attendee_count']} others]" if e.get("attendee_count") else ""
   288→            lines.append(f"- {e['title']} ({time_str}){location}{attendees}")
   289→
   290→    if recent:
   291→        lines.append("### Recent Events (past week)")
   292→        for start_dt, e in recent[:5]:
   293→            if e.get("is_all_day"):
   294→                time_str = start_dt.strftime("%a %b %d (all day)")
   295→            else:
   296→                time_str = start_dt.strftime("%a %b %d at %I:%M%p").replace(" 0", " ")
   297→            location = f" at {e['location']}" if e.get("location") else ""
   298→            lines.append(f"- {e['title']} ({time_str}){location}")
   299→
   300→    if not lines:
   301→        return ""
   302→
   303→    lines.append(
   304→        "\nNote: Use search_calendar to look up more events or search by keyword/date."
   305→    )
   306→
   307→    return "\n".join(lines)
   308→
   309→
   310→def build_system_prompt(
   311→    user_profile: dict,
   312→    purchases: list[dict],
   313→    purchase_stats: dict | None = None,
   314→    calendar_events: list[dict] | None = None,
   315→    session_history: list[dict] | None = None,
   316→    session_state: dict | None = None,
   317→) -> str:
   318→    """Build the full system prompt with user data injected."""
   319→    parts = [MIRA_PERSONALITY]
   320→
   321→    # User profile
   322→    parts.append("\n## User Profile")
   323→    name = user_profile.get("name", "this person")
   324→    parts.append(f"Name: {name}")
   325→
   326→    brands = user_profile.get("brands", [])
   327→    if brands:
   328→        parts.append(f"Favorite brands: {', '.join(brands)}")
   329→
   330→    price_range = user_profile.get("price_range")
   331→    if price_range:
   332→        parts.append(
   333→            f"Price range: ${price_range.get('min', '?')}-${price_range.get('max', '?')} "
   334→            f"(avg ${price_range.get('avg', '?')})"
   335→        )
   336→
   337→    style_tags = user_profile.get("style_tags", [])
   338→    if style_tags:
   339→        parts.append(f"Style: {', '.join(style_tags)}")
   340→
   341→    narrative = user_profile.get("narrative_summary")
   342→    if narrative:
   343→        parts.append(f"Style narrative: {narrative}")
   344→
   345→    # Purchase statistics (aggregate view of full history)
   346→    if purchase_stats:
   347→        stats_section = _format_purchase_stats(purchase_stats)
   348→        if stats_section:
   349→            parts.append(f"\n{stats_section}")
   350→
   351→    # Tiered purchases — filtered to fashion items, then displayed by recency
   352→    filtered_purchases = _filter_fashion_purchases(purchases)
   353→    non_fashion_count = sum(1 for p in purchases if not p.get("is_fashion", True))
   354→    if filtered_purchases:
   355→        tiered = _build_tiered_purchases(filtered_purchases)
   356→        if tiered:
   357→            parts.append(f"\n## Purchase History (Tiered)")
   358→            parts.append(tiered)
   359→        else:
   360→            parts.append("\n## Purchase History")
   361→            parts.append("Purchases exist but could not be categorized by date.")
   362→        if non_fashion_count > 0:
   363→            parts.append(
   364→                f"\nNote: {non_fashion_count} non-fashion purchases also in history "
   365→                "(use search_purchases to explore)."
   366→            )
   367→    else:
   368→        parts.append("\n## Purchase History")
   369→        parts.append(
   370→            "No purchase history available. For Turn 1, skip the purchase roast — instead, "
   371→            "open by roasting their current outfit from the camera snapshot with maximum "
   372→            "personality. Comment on what you see and make it funny. Then transition into "
   373→            "recommendations assertively: 'I already know what you need, hold on.' "
   374→            "Do NOT ask about style preferences — just start pulling items based on what you see."
   375→        )
   376→
   377→    # Calendar events — injected between purchases and sessions for context layering
   378→    if calendar_events:
   379→        cal_section = _format_calendar_events(calendar_events)
   380→        if cal_section:
   381→            parts.append("\n## Calendar")
   382→            parts.append(cal_section)
   383→
   384→    # Past session memory
   385→    if session_history:
   386→        parts.append("\n## Past Sessions")
   387→        for session in session_history[-3:]:  # Last 3 sessions
   388→            parts.append(f"- {session.get('summary', 'No summary')}")
   389→            liked = session.get("liked_items", [])
   390→            if liked:
   391→                names = [item.get("title", "?") for item in liked[:3]]
   392→                parts.append(f"  Liked: {', '.join(names)}")
   393→
   394→    # Current session state
   395→    if session_state:
   396→        parts.append("\n## Current Session")
   397→        items_shown = session_state.get("items_shown", 0)
   398→        likes = session_state.get("likes", 0)
   399→        dislikes = session_state.get("dislikes", 0)
   400→        api_calls = session_state.get("api_calls", 0)
   401→        parts.append(f"Items shown: {items_shown}, Likes: {likes}, Dislikes: {dislikes}")
   402→        if api_calls >= 18:
   403→            parts.append("NOTE: You're approaching the session limit. Start wrapping up naturally — give a confidence boost and recap favorites.")
   404→
   405→    return "\n".join(parts)

   408→# --- Recommendation Pipeline Prompts ---
   409→
   410→MIRA_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are Mira, an AI fashion stylist for the Mirrorless smart mirror.
   411→
   412→**Personality**: You're friendly, teasing, and fashion-savvy. You know your user's shopping history intimately and reference specific purchases to show you're paying attention. You're encouraging but honest about what works.
   413→
   414→**Your Task**: Generate exactly 5 personalized outfit recommendations based on the user's recent shopping history. Be concise in your descriptions.
   415→
   416→**How to work**:
   417→1. Read the user's profile, purchase history, and style preferences provided in the message.
   418→2. Review the available clothing items (tops and bottoms) provided in the message.
   419→3. Pick the best combinations and return your final outfit JSON.
   420→
   421→**Guidelines**:
   422→1. **Match their existing style** - Don't push them outside their comfort zone. Look at what they've bought recently and suggest similar items.
   423→2. **Each outfit = TOP + BOTTOM minimum** - Can add accessories/shoes if relevant
   424→3. **Be specific** - Reference exact purchases with dates when explaining why items work together
   425→4. **Consider season** - Today is {current_date}
   426→5. **Budget awareness** - Most recommendations should be in their usual price range, but you can suggest 1-2 splurge items if they make sense
   427→6. **Make it personal** - Use patterns you notice ("You've bought 3 navy tops in the last month...", "This would go great with those gray pants you got in December")
   428→7. **Brand diversity** - MIX brands across outfits. Do NOT recommend all items from the same brand. Each outfit should ideally have items from DIFFERENT brands. Across all 5 outfits, use at LEAST 4 different brands.
   429→8. **Style diversity** - Each outfit should have a DIFFERENT vibe and use DIFFERENT item types. Mix sweaters, hoodies, jackets, long-sleeves — NOT all t-shirts. Mix jeans, chinos, joggers — NOT the same pants repeated. NEVER reuse the same item across outfits.
   430→
   431→**Output Format** (JSON — return ONLY this JSON, no other text):
   432→```json
   433→{{{{
   434→  "greeting": "Hey [name]! I see you've been on a [pattern] kick lately...",
   435→  "style_analysis": "Brief analysis of their shopping patterns (1-2 sentences)",
   436→  "outfits": [
   437→    {{{{
   438→      "outfit_name": "Casual Friday Vibes",
   439→      "description": "Why this combination works and what vibe it gives",
   440→      "items": [
   441→        {{{{
   442→          "type": "top",
   443→          "item": {{{{
   444→            "title": "Item name exactly as shown in available_clothing",
   445→            "source": "Brand/Seller name",
   446→            "price": "$XX.XX",
   447→            "price_numeric": 29.99,
   448→            "image_url": "https://...",
   449→            "link": "https://...",
   450→            "product_id": "abc123",
   451→            "rating": 4.5
   452→          }}}}
   453→        }}}},
   454→        {{{{
   455→          "type": "bottom",
   456→          "item": {{{{
   457→            "title": "Item name exactly as shown in available_clothing",
   458→            "source": "Brand/Seller name",
   459→            "price": "$XX.XX",
   460→            "price_numeric": 29.99,
   461→            "image_url": "https://...",
   462→            "link": "https://...",
   463→            "product_id": "abc123",
   464→            "rating": 4.5
   465→          }}}}
   466→        }}}}
   467→      ],
   468→      "why_its_a_match": "One sentence explaining why this outfit suits the user based on their style/history",
   469→      "mira_comment": "A teasing or encouraging personal comment about this outfit"
   470→    }}}}
   471→  ]
   472→}}}}
   473→```
   474→
   475→**CRITICAL rules**:
   476→- Copy item fields EXACTLY as they appear in the tool results. Do NOT rename fields (e.g. do NOT use "name" instead of "title", do NOT use "brand" instead of "source", do NOT use "image" instead of "image_url").
   477→- EVERY outfit MUST have at least 2 items (top + bottom). Single-item outfits are NOT acceptable.
   478→- Each outfit should have 2-4 items (minimum top + bottom)
   479→- Pick tops ONLY from the TOPS section and bottoms ONLY from the BOTTOMS section. Do NOT invent items or use items from the wrong section.
   480→- ONLY use items that appear in the tool results. NEVER fabricate items that aren't listed.
   481→- Your comments should feel like they're from someone who knows the user's closet
   482→- If you can't find good matches, explain why in-character
   483→"""
   484→

   486→def build_user_context_prompt(user_data: Dict) -> str:
   487→    """
   488→    Build user message with profile + purchase context only (no clothing items).
   489→
   490→    Used with the tool-calling flow where Claude calls give_recommendation
   491→    to fetch clothing items itself.
   492→    """
   493→    user = user_data["user"]
   494→    style_profile = user_data.get("style_profile")
   495→    recent_purchases = user_data.get("recent_purchases", [])
   496→    top_brands = user_data.get("top_brands", [])
   497→
   498→    # Format recent purchases
   499→    purchases_text = ""
   500→    if recent_purchases:
   501→        purchases_text = "**Recent Purchases (last 6 months)**:\n"
   502→        for p in recent_purchases[:15]:
   503→            raw_date = p.get("date")
   504→            if raw_date is None:
   505→                date_str = "Unknown date"
   506→            elif isinstance(raw_date, str):
   507→                date_str = datetime.strptime(raw_date, "%Y-%m-%d").strftime("%B %Y")
   508→            else:
   509→                date_str = raw_date.strftime("%B %Y")
   510→            price_str = f"${float(p['price']):.2f}" if p.get("price") else "Unknown price"
   511→            purchases_text += f"- {p['item_name']} by {p['brand']} ({date_str}, {price_str})\n"
   512→    else:
   513→        purchases_text = "**No recent purchases found** - this is a new user\n"
   514→
   515→    # Format style profile
   516→    style_text = ""
   517→    if style_profile:
   518→        style_text = f"""
   519→**Style Profile**:
   520→- Favorite brands: {', '.join(style_profile.get('brands', []))}
   521→- Price range: ${style_profile.get('price_range', {}).get('min', 0)} - ${style_profile.get('price_range', {}).get('max', 500)}
   522→- Style tags: {', '.join(style_profile.get('style_tags', []))}
   523→"""
   524→    else:
   525→        style_text = "**No style profile available**\n"
   526→
   527→    # Determine gender for tool hint
   528→    gender = "mens"
   529→    if style_profile:
   530→        gender = style_profile.get("gender", "mens")
   531→
   532→    prompt = f"""
   533→**User**: {user['name']} ({user['email']})
   534→
   535→{style_text}
   536→
   537→{purchases_text}
   538→
   539→**Top 5 Favorite Brands**: {', '.join(top_brands) if top_brands else 'None yet'}
   540→
   541→---
   542→
   543→Please use the `give_recommendation` tool to search for clothing items, then generate 5-7 outfit recommendations for {user['name']}.
   544→Use gender="{gender}" and include their favorite brands: {', '.join(top_brands[:3]) if top_brands else 'pick popular brands'}.
   545→Be specific and reference their purchase history!
   546→"""
   547→
   548→    return prompt
   549→
   550→
   551→def build_recommendation_prompt(user_data: Dict, available_clothing: List[Dict]) -> str:
   552→    """
   553→    Build user message with context (purchases + available items).
   554→
   555→    Args:
   556→        user_data: Dict with user, style_profile, recent_purchases, top_brands
   557→        available_clothing: List of clothing items from Serper
   558→
   559→    Returns:
   560→        Formatted prompt string with all context
   561→    """
   562→    user = user_data["user"]
   563→    style_profile = user_data.get("style_profile")
   564→    recent_purchases = user_data.get("recent_purchases", [])
   565→    top_brands = user_data.get("top_brands", [])
   566→
   567→    # Format recent purchases
   568→    purchases_text = ""
   569→    if recent_purchases:
   570→        purchases_text = "**Recent Purchases (last 6 months)**:\n"
   571→        for p in recent_purchases[:15]:  # Limit to 15 most recent
   572→            raw_date = p.get("date")
   573→            if raw_date is None:
   574→                date_str = "Unknown date"
   575→            elif isinstance(raw_date, str):
   576→                date_str = datetime.strptime(raw_date, "%Y-%m-%d").strftime("%B %Y")
   577→            else:
   578→                date_str = raw_date.strftime("%B %Y")
   579→            price_str = f"${float(p['price']):.2f}" if p.get("price") else "Unknown price"
   580→            purchases_text += f"- {p['item_name']} by {p['brand']} ({date_str}, {price_str})\n"
   581→    else:
   582→        purchases_text = "**No recent purchases found** - this is a new user\n"
   583→
   584→    # Format style profile
   585→    style_text = ""
   586→    if style_profile:
   587→        price_range = style_profile.get('price_range') or {}
   588→        style_text = f"""
   589→**Style Profile**:
   590→- Favorite brands: {', '.join(style_profile.get('brands', []))}
   591→- Price range: ${price_range.get('min', 0)} - ${price_range.get('max', 500)}
   592→- Style tags: {', '.join(style_profile.get('style_tags', []))}
   593→"""
   594→    else:
   595→        style_text = "**No style profile available**\n"
   596→
   597→    # Format available clothing, grouped by category
   598→    tops = [i for i in available_clothing if i.get("clothing_category") == "top"]
   599→    bottoms = [i for i in available_clothing if i.get("clothing_category") == "bottom"]
   600→    uncategorized = [i for i in available_clothing if not i.get("clothing_category")]
   601→
   602→    def _format_items(items):
   603→        text = ""
   604→        for item in items:
   605→            text += f"""
   606→- **{item['title']}**
   607→  - Brand/Seller: {item['source']}
   608→  - Price: {item['price']}
   609→  - Rating: {item.get('rating', 'N/A')}
   610→  - Link: {item['link']}
   611→  - Image: {item['image_url']}
   612→  - Product ID: {item['product_id']}
   613→"""
   614→        return text
   615→
   616→    clothing_text = f"\n**Available Clothing Items ({len(available_clothing)} items)**:\n"
   617→    clothing_text += "IMPORTANT: Pick tops ONLY from the TOPS section and bottoms ONLY from the BOTTOMS section.\n"
   618→    if tops:
   619→        clothing_text += f"\n### TOPS ({len(tops)} items) — use these for the \"top\" slot:\n"
   620→        clothing_text += _format_items(tops)
   621→    if bottoms:
   622→        clothing_text += f"\n### BOTTOMS ({len(bottoms)} items) — use these for the \"bottom\" slot:\n"
   623→        clothing_text += _format_items(bottoms)
   624→    if uncategorized:
   625→        clothing_text += f"\n### OTHER ({len(uncategorized)} items):\n"
   626→        clothing_text += _format_items(uncategorized)
   627→
   628→    prompt = f"""
   629→**User**: {user['name']} ({user['email']})
   630→
   631→{style_text}
   632→
   633→{purchases_text}
   634→
   635→**Top 5 Favorite Brands**: {', '.join(top_brands) if top_brands else 'None yet'}
   636→
   637→{clothing_text}
   638→
   639→---
   640→
   641→Now generate exactly 5 outfit recommendations for {user['name']}. Keep descriptions brief (1 sentence each). Be specific and reference their purchase history!
   642→"""
   643→
   644→    return prompt
   645→
   646→
   647→def get_mira_system_prompt() -> str:
   648→    """Get the Mira system prompt with current date."""
   649→    current_date = datetime.now().strftime("%B %d, %Y")
   650→    return MIRA_SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(current_date=current_date)

content.text:
Perfect! I have found comprehensive information about the silence detection mechanism. Let me compile the complete report.

## Search Results: Silence Detection & Re-prompt Mechanism

I found a **silence detection and re-prompt system** in your codebase. Here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. **Backend Silence Detection** (`backend/agent/orchestrator.py`)

**Key Constants:**
- **Line 42**: `SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5` — Silence is detected after 5 seconds of no user input

**Core Mechanism:**

- **Lines 172-173**: When a session starts, `_start_silence_timer(user_id)` is called to begin monitoring user silence
  
- **Lines 620-633**: The `_start_silence_timer()` method:
  - Creates an async task that sleeps for `SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`
  - If the session is still active and not currently processing, fires a `silence` event
  - The event contains: `{"type": "silence", "duration_seconds": 5}`

- **Lines 635-639**: `_cancel_silence_timer(user_id)` cancels the timer when:
  - User provides input (voice, gesture, pose, etc.)
  - Session ends via `end_session()`
  - Graceful shutdown is triggered

**Event Handling:**

- **Lines 187-251**: The `handle_event()` method processes silence events:
  - **Line 208**: Non-silence events (voice, gesture, etc.) restart the silence timer
  - **Line 595-597**: Silence events are converted to a Claude message: `"The user has been silent for {duration} seconds. Say something to keep the conversation going."`
  - This triggers a new Claude API call with the silence prompt

### 2. **No 3-Attempt Limit Found**

**Important:** I did NOT find any hardcoded limit that fires off after **3 silence events** or tracks `prompt_count` / `silence_count`. The system will keep firing silence events indefinitely as long as the session is active (no explicit limit).

### 3. **Graceful Shutdown** (`backend/agent/orchestrator.py`)

- **Lines 491-513**: When API call count reaches `SOFT_API_LIMIT` (line 44: `= 20`), a graceful shutdown is triggered
- This is NOT related to silence — it's based on API call count, not silence events
- **Line 282-284**: Session enters `wrapping_up = True` state and no more events are processed

### 4. **Mira Personality on Silence** (`backend/agent/prompts.py`)

- **Lines 8-106**: `MIRA_PERSONALITY` prompt includes guidance for handling silence:
  - **Line 20**: "When filling silence: 'So are we gonna talk about those 3 ASOS orders in one week or...'"
  - The prompt tells Claude to reference purchase history when the user is silent
  - No explicit instruction for a 3-attempt limit

### 5. **Frontend: 2-Minute Waiting State Timeout** (`frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`)

- **Lines 32-33**: `WAITING_TIMEOUT_MS = 2 * 60 * 1000` — Different timeout in waiting state (before session starts)
- **Lines 152-166**: In the waiting state, if the user doesn't click "Start Session" within 2 minutes, they are auto-skipped
- This is NOT related to in-session silence detection

---

## Summary

| Feature | Location | Details |
|---------|----------|---------|
| **Silence Detection** | `orchestrator.py:620-633` | Fires after 5 seconds of no input |
| **Silence Event Type** | `orchestrator.py:595-597` | Converted to Claude message: "user silent for X seconds" |
| **Timer Reset** | `orchestrator.py:205-210` | Resets on any user input (voice, gesture, pose) |
| **3-Attempt Limit** | ❌ NOT FOUND | No hardcoded limit exists |
| **Prompt Count** | ❌ NOT FOUND | No `prompt_count` or `silence_count` tracking |
| **API Limit** | `orchestrator.py:44, 280-284` | API limit = 20 calls (unrelated to silence) |
| **Mira's Silence Response** | `prompts.py:20` | References purchase history during silence |

**The silence detection will keep re-prompting indefinitely** (as long as the user stays silent and the session remains active) unless you implement a hard limit elsewhere.

content.text:
Good news — the silence detection **does exist** in `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`. Here's what's currently implemented:

- **5-second timer** (`SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5` at line 42) — fires a `silence` event when the user hasn't spoken
- **Timer resets** on any input (voice, gesture, pose)
- **Silence prompt** sent to Claude: *"The user has been silent for X seconds. Say something to keep the conversation going."*

**However — no 3-attempt limit was ever implemented.** The silence events will keep firing indefinitely as long as the session is active. So if the user stays quiet, Mira will just keep nudging forever.

The only related limit is `SOFT_API_LIMIT = 20` (line 44), which triggers a graceful session shutdown after 20 total Claude API calls — but that's not silence-specific.

1→"""Mira — Event-driven AI agent orchestrator.
     2→
     3→Receives events (voice, gestures, poses, silence) and orchestrates Claude API
     4→calls with tool use. Streams responses to HeyGen for voice output and broadcasts
     5→tool results to the frontend via Socket.io.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→import json
    10→import os
    11→import time
    12→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    13→from typing import Dict, Optional
    14→from uuid import uuid4
    15→
    16→import anthropic
    17→from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic
    18→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    19→
    20→from agent.prompts import build_system_prompt, build_recommendation_prompt, get_mira_system_prompt
    21→from agent.tools import TOOL_DEFINITIONS, execute_tool
    22→from agent.memory import (
    23→    load_user_profile,
    24→    load_user_purchases,
    25→    load_past_sessions,
    26→    load_purchase_statistics,
    27→    save_session_summary,
    28→    get_user_oauth_token,
    29→    refresh_calendar_events,
    30→)
    31→from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
    32→from services.user_data_service import (
    33→    get_user_profile_and_purchases,
    34→    is_new_user,
    35→    save_outfits_to_database,
    36→)
    37→
    38→load_dotenv()
    39→
    40→SONNET_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"
    41→HAIKU_MODEL = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
    42→SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
    43→EVENT_BATCH_WINDOW_MS = 200
    44→SOFT_API_LIMIT = 20
    45→
    46→# Initialize Anthropic client for recommendation pipeline
    47→anthropic_client = AsyncAnthropic(api_key=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))
    48→
    49→
    50→@dataclass
    51→class SessionState:
    52→    """Tracks state for a single mirror session."""
    53→
    54→    session_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()))
    55→    user_id: str = ""
    56→    api_calls: int = 0
    57→    items_shown: int = 0
    58→    likes: int = 0
    59→    dislikes: int = 0
    60→    liked_items: list = field(default_factory=list)
    61→    conversation_history: list = field(default_factory=list)
    62→    is_active: bool = True
    63→    last_input_time: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    64→    wrapping_up: bool = False
    65→    is_processing: bool = False
    66→    user_context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    67→    system_prompt: str = ""
    68→    _last_shown_item: dict | None = None
    69→    _event_lock: asyncio.Lock = field(default_factory=asyncio.Lock)
    70→
    71→
    72→class MiraOrchestrator:
    73→    """Event-driven orchestrator for the Mira AI stylist agent."""
    74→
    75→    def __init__(self, socket_io=None):
    76→        self.client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(
    77→            auth_token=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN"),
    78→            default_headers={"anthropic-beta": "oauth-2025-04-20"},
    79→            default_query={"beta": "true"},
    80→        )
    81→        self.sio = socket_io
    82→        self.sessions: dict[str, SessionState] = {}
    83→        self._silence_tasks: dict[str, asyncio.Task] = {}
    84→
    85→    async def start_session(self, user_id: str) -> SessionState:
    86→        """Initialize a new Mira session for a user."""
    87→        # End any existing session to prevent orphaned timers
    88→        if user_id in self.sessions:
    89→            await self.end_session(user_id)
    90→
    91→        session = SessionState(user_id=user_id)
    92→        self.sessions[user_id] = session
    93→
    94→        # Load user data from DB in parallel — fall back to empty defaults if any fail
    95→        profile = {}
    96→        purchases = []
    97→        past_sessions = []
    98→        oauth_token = None
    99→        purchase_stats = {}
   100→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   101→        try:
   102→            profile, purchases, past_sessions, oauth_token, purchase_stats = await asyncio.gather(
   103→                load_user_profile(db, user_id),
   104→                load_user_purchases(db, user_id),
   105→                load_past_sessions(db, user_id),
   106→                get_user_oauth_token(db, user_id),
   107→                load_purchase_statistics(db, user_id),
   108→            )
   109→        except Exception as e:
   110→            print(f"[mira] Failed to load user data for {user_id}, using defaults: {e}")
   111→        finally:
   112→            await db.close()
   113→
   114→        # Phase 2: Refresh calendar events from Google API (needs oauth_token from phase 1)
   115→        calendar_events = []
   116→        if oauth_token:
   117→            db = NeonHTTPClient()
   118→            try:
   119→                calendar_events = await refresh_calendar_events(db, user_id, oauth_token)
   120→                print(f"[mira] Loaded {len(calendar_events)} calendar events for {user_id}")
   121→            except Exception as e:
   122→                print(f"[mira] Calendar refresh failed for {user_id}, continuing without: {e}")
   123→            finally:
   124→                await db.close()
   125→
   126→        # Store on session for tool access
   127→        session.user_context = {
   128→            "profile": profile,
   129→            "purchases": purchases,
   130→            "past_sessions": past_sessions,
   131→            "oauth_token": oauth_token,
   132→            "purchase_stats": purchase_stats,
   133→            "calendar_events": calendar_events,
   134→        }
   135→
   136→        # Build system prompt with all user data
   137→        session.system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   138→            user_profile=profile,
   139→            purchases=purchases,
   140→            purchase_stats=purchase_stats,
   141→            calendar_events=calendar_events,
   142→            session_history=past_sessions,
   143→            session_state={
   144→                "items_shown": 0,
   145→                "likes": 0,
   146→                "dislikes": 0,
   147→                "api_calls": 0,
   148→            },
   149→        )
   150→
   151→        # Emit system prompt for debugging
   152→        if self.sio:
   153→            await self.sio.emit(
   154→                "debug_system_prompt",
   155→                {"prompt": session.system_prompt},
   156→                room=user_id,
   157→            )
   158→
   159→        # Create the DB session record (best-effort)
   160→        try:
   161→            db = NeonHTTPClient()
   162→            try:
   163→                await db.execute(
   164→                    "INSERT INTO sessions (id, user_id, started_at, status) "
   165→                    "VALUES ($1, $2::uuid, now(), 'active')",
   166→                    [session.session_id, user_id],
   167→                )
   168→            finally:
   169→                await db.close()
   170→        except Exception as e:
   171→            print(f"[mira] Failed to insert session record for {user_id}: {e}")
   172→
   173→        # Start silence detection
   174→        self._start_silence_timer(user_id)
   175→
   176→        # Trigger Mira's opening line
   177→        await self.handle_event(user_id, {
   178→            "type": "session_start",
   179→            "message": "A new user just stepped up to the mirror. Introduce yourself and start the session.",
   180→        })
   181→
   182→        # Request a snapshot from the mirror so Mira can see the user
   183→        if self.sio:
   184→            await self.sio.emit("request_snapshot", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
   185→
   186→        return session
   187→
   188→    async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
   189→        """Handle an incoming event and generate Mira's response.
   190→
   191→        Events are: voice, gesture (swipe_right, swipe_left, thumbs_up, thumbs_down),
   192→        pose, snapshot, silence, session_start.
   193→        """
   194→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   195→        if not session or not session.is_active:
   196→            print(f"[mira] handle_event: no active session for {user_id}, ignoring event {event.get('type', '?')}")
   197→            return
   198→
   199→        # Block all new events once graceful shutdown has started
   200→        if session.wrapping_up:

   204→        async with session._event_lock:

   240→                        "likes": session.likes,
   241→                        "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   242→                        "api_calls": session.api_calls,
   243→                    },
   244→                )
   245→
   246→                # Call Claude
   247→                try:
   248→                    await self._call_claude(session)
   249→                except Exception as e:
   250→                    print(f"[mira] Error calling Claude: {e}")
   251→                    await self._stream_text(user_id, "Hmm, my brain glitched for a second. What were we talking about?")
   252→                    await self._stream_text(user_id, "", end_of_message=True)
   253→            finally:
   254→                session.is_processing = False
   255→
   256→    def _select_model(self, session: SessionState) -> tuple[str, int]:
   257→        """Select the right model and max_tokens for this turn.
   258→
   259→        Returns (model_id, max_tokens).
   260→        - Sonnet for conversational turns (speech quality matters)
   261→        - Haiku for tool-result processing (speed matters)
   262→        """
   263→        if session.conversation_history:
   264→            last_msg = session.conversation_history[-1]
   265→            # tool_result messages have list content with type: "tool_result" dicts
   266→            if last_msg.get("role") == "user" and isinstance(last_msg.get("content"), list):
   267→                if any(
   268→                    isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_result"
   269→                    for block in last_msg["content"]
   270→                ):
   271→                    return HAIKU_MODEL, 2048
   272→        return SONNET_MODEL, 400
   273→
   274→    def _validate_history(self, session: SessionState) -> None:
   275→        """Validate conversation history and truncate to last valid point if corrupted.
   276→
   277→        Checks:
   278→        1. Alternating user → assistant → user roles (tool_result messages are user role)
   279→        2. Every assistant message with tool_use blocks is immediately followed by a
   280→           user message with matching tool_result blocks
   281→
   282→        If corruption is found, truncates history to the last valid position and logs
   283→        a warning. This is defense-in-depth — the event lock should prevent corruption,
   284→        but edge cases or future bugs could still cause issues.
   285→        """
   286→        history = session.conversation_history
   287→        if not history:
   288→            return
   289→
   290→        valid_up_to = 0  # exclusive — history[:valid_up_to] is valid
   291→
   292→        i = 0
   293→        while i < len(history):
   294→            msg = history[i]
   295→            role = msg.get("role")
   296→
   297→            # First message must be from user
   298→            if i == 0 and role != "user":
   299→                print(f"[mira] History validation: first message is {role}, expected user — truncating")
   300→                break
   301→
   302→            # Check alternating roles (user and assistant)
   303→            if i > 0:
   304→                prev_role = history[i - 1].get("role")
   305→                # After user, expect assistant; after assistant, expect user
   306→                if prev_role == role and role != "user":
   307→                    # Two consecutive assistant messages — corruption
   308→                    print(f"[mira] History validation: consecutive {role} messages at index {i} — truncating")
   309→                    break
   310→                if prev_role == "user" and role == "user":
   311→                    # Two consecutive user messages — corruption (unless first is tool_result)
   312→                    # A tool_result user message followed by a regular user message is invalid
   313→                    print(f"[mira] History validation: consecutive user messages at index {i} — truncating")
   314→                    break
   315→
   316→            # If this is an assistant message with tool_use, verify next message has tool_results
   317→            if role == "assistant":
   318→                content = msg.get("content", [])
   319→                has_tool_use = False
   320→                if isinstance(content, list):
   321→                    has_tool_use = any(
   322→                        (getattr(block, "type", None) == "tool_use")
   323→                        or (isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_use")
   324→                        for block in content
   325→                    )
   326→
   327→                if has_tool_use:
   328→                    # Must be followed by a user message with tool_result blocks
   329→                    if i + 1 >= len(history):
   330→                        print(f"[mira] History validation: tool_use at index {i} with no following tool_result — truncating")
   331→                        break
   332→                    next_msg = history[i + 1]
   333→                    if next_msg.get("role") != "user":
   334→                        print(f"[mira] History validation: tool_use at index {i} followed by {next_msg.get('role')} — truncating")
   335→                        break
   336→                    next_content = next_msg.get("content", [])
   337→                    has_tool_result = isinstance(next_content, list) and any(
   338→                        isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_result"
   339→                        for block in next_content
   340→                    )
   341→                    if not has_tool_result:
   342→                        print(f"[mira] History validation: tool_use at index {i} followed by user message without tool_result — truncating")
   343→                        break
   344→
   345→            valid_up_to = i + 1
   346→            i += 1
   347→
   348→        if valid_up_to < len(history):
   349→            removed = len(history) - valid_up_to
   350→            session.conversation_history = history[:valid_up_to]
   351→            print(f"[mira] History validation: removed {removed} messages, kept {valid_up_to} for {session.user_id}")
   352→
   353→    async def _call_claude(self, session: SessionState, tool_depth: int = 0) -> None:
   354→        """Make a streaming Claude API call with tool use."""
   355→        if session.wrapping_up:
   356→            return
   357→
   358→        if tool_depth >= 3:
   359→            print(f"[mira] Tool depth limit reached ({tool_depth}) for {session.user_id}, stopping")
   360→            return
   361→
   362→        if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
   363→            print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}, initiating graceful shutdown")
   364→            if not session.wrapping_up:
   365→                session.wrapping_up = True
   366→                await self._graceful_shutdown(session)
   367→            return
   368→
   369→        # Defense-in-depth: validate history before sending to Claude
   370→        self._validate_history(session)
   371→
   372→        session.api_calls += 1
   373→        model, max_tokens = self._select_model(session)
   374→        print(f"[mira] Using {model} (max_tokens={max_tokens}) for {session.user_id}")
   375→
   376→        # Collect full response (streaming to frontend happens via callback)
   377→        collected_text = ""
   378→        tool_uses = []
   379→        print(f"[mira] Calling Claude for {session.user_id} (turn #{session.api_calls})...")
   380→
   381→        try:
   382→            async with self.client.messages.stream(
   383→                model=model,
   384→                max_tokens=max_tokens,
   385→                system=session.system_prompt,
   386→                messages=session.conversation_history,
   387→                tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
   388→            ) as stream:
   389→                async for event in stream:
   390→                    if event.type == "content_block_delta":
   391→                        if hasattr(event.delta, "text"):
   392→                            collected_text += event.delta.text
   393→                            # Stream each chunk to HeyGen / frontend
   394→                            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, event.delta.text)
   395→
   396→                    elif event.type == "content_block_stop":
   397→                        pass
   398→
   399→                # Get the final message for tool use blocks
   400→                final_message = await stream.get_final_message()
   401→
   402→            # Signal end-of-message so frontend flushes the sentence buffer
   403→            if collected_text:
   404→                print(f"[mira] AGENT SAID: {collected_text}")
   405→                await self._stream_text(session.user_id, "", end_of_message=True)
   406→        except Exception as e:
   407→            print(f"[mira] Claude API call failed for {session.user_id}: {e}")
   408→            # Pop the last user message to keep conversation history consistent
   409→            if session.conversation_history and session.conversation_history[-1]["role"] == "user":
   410→                session.conversation_history.pop()
   411→            # Also pop any orphaned assistant message with tool_use blocks
   412→            # (prevents permanent 400 errors from unmatched tool_use/tool_result pairs)
   413→            if (
   414→                session.conversation_history
   415→                and session.conversation_history[-1].get("role") == "assistant"
   416→            ):
   417→                last_content = session.conversation_history[-1].get("content", [])
   418→                has_tool_use = any(
   419→                    getattr(block, "type", None) == "tool_use"
   420→                    or (isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_use")
   421→                    for block in (last_content if isinstance(last_content, list) else [])
   422→                )
   423→                if has_tool_use:
   424→                    session.conversation_history.pop()
   425→            # Emit a fallback message so the frontend user gets verbal feedback
   426→            fallback = "Hmm, my brain glitched for a second. What were we talking about?"
   427→            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, fallback)
   428→            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, "", end_of_message=True)
   429→            return
   430→
   431→        # Process any tool use blocks
   432→        for block in final_message.content:
   433→            if block.type == "tool_use":
   434→                tool_uses.append(block)
   435→
   436→        # Add assistant message to history
   437→        session.conversation_history.append({
   438→            "role": "assistant",
   439→            "content": final_message.content,
   440→        })
   441→
   442→        # Handle tool calls
   443→        if tool_uses:
   444→            await self._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth)
   445→
   446→    async def _handle_tool_calls(self, session: SessionState, tool_uses: list, tool_depth: int = 0) -> None:
   447→        """Execute tool calls and continue the conversation."""
   448→        tool_results = []
   449→
   450→        for tool_use in tool_uses:
   451→            # Log tool call with truncated input for terminal visibility
   452→            input_str = json.dumps(tool_use.input)
   453→            if len(input_str) > 200:
   454→                input_str = input_str[:200] + "..."
   455→            print(f"[mira] Tool call: {tool_use.name}({input_str})")
   456→
   457→            try:
   458→                result = await execute_tool(
   459→                    tool_name=tool_use.name,
   460→                    tool_input=tool_use.input,
   461→                    user_context={
   462→                        "user_id": session.user_id,
   463→                        "session_id": session.session_id,
   464→                        "oauth_token": session.user_context.get("oauth_token"),
   465→                    },
   466→                )
   467→            except Exception as e:
   468→                print(f"[mira] Tool {tool_use.name} failed: {e}")
   469→                result = {"error": f"Tool execution failed: {str(e)}"}
   470→
   471→            # Parallel broadcast: send results to frontend via Socket.io
   472→            frontend_payload = result.pop("frontend_payload", None)
   473→            if frontend_payload and self.sio:
   474→                await self.sio.emit(
   475→                    "tool_result",
   476→                    frontend_payload,
   477→                    room=session.user_id,
   478→                )
   479→
   480→            # Track items shown (present_items + display_product count — search_clothing is invisible)
   481→            if tool_use.name in ("present_items", "display_product") and result.get("items"):
   482→                session.items_shown += len(result["items"])
   483→                if result["items"]:
   484→                    session._last_shown_item = result["items"][0]
   485→
   486→            tool_result_block = {
   487→                "type": "tool_result",
   488→                "tool_use_id": tool_use.id,
   489→                "content": json.dumps(result),
   490→            }
   491→            if "error" in result:
   492→                tool_result_block["is_error"] = True
   493→            tool_results.append(tool_result_block)
   494→
   495→        # Add tool results to conversation and continue
   496→        session.conversation_history.append({
   497→            "role": "user",
   498→            "content": tool_results,
   499→        })

data.prompt:
The user reports that in the first ~3 prompts of a session, Mira's TTS audio gets "cut off" badly, but after that it works fine. This suggests there's some interrupt/barge-in mechanism or counter that controls when audio can be interrupted.

Search VERY thoroughly for anything related to:
1. Audio interruption, barge-in, cutting off TTS playback
2. Any counter that tracks how many times something has been interrupted or how many prompts have been sent
3. Keywords: "interrupt", "barge", "cut", "cancel", "abort", "stop", "count", "attempt", "first", "initial", "warmup", "ready"
4. MediaSource or SourceBuffer initialization that might cause early audio drops
5. Any logic that behaves differently for the first N messages vs later ones
6. Any flag or counter in the video avatar hook that tracks speaking state transitions

Check these files thoroughly:
- frontend/src/lib/streaming-tts.ts (the TTS playback system)
- frontend/src/hooks/useMiraVideoAvatar.ts (avatar state machine - speaking state)
- frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx (where TTS is triggered, mira_speech handler)
- frontend/src/hooks/useDeepgramSTT.ts (STT - could trigger interrupts when hearing audio feedback)
- backend/agent/orchestrator.py (any interrupt or silence event logic)

Also search for any "fire_off" or "silent fire off" pattern — something that auto-sends a prompt without user input.

Report ALL findings with exact file paths and line numbers. Include any counters, timers, or state variables that could explain why the first 3 responses behave differently.

   188→    async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
   189→        """Handle an incoming event and generate Mira's response.
   190→
   191→        Events are: voice, gesture (swipe_right, swipe_left, thumbs_up, thumbs_down),
   192→        pose, snapshot, silence, session_start.
   193→        """
   194→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   195→        if not session or not session.is_active:
   196→            print(f"[mira] handle_event: no active session for {user_id}, ignoring event {event.get('type', '?')}")
   197→            return

500→
   501→        # Call Claude again to process tool results
   502→        await self._call_claude(session, tool_depth=tool_depth + 1)
   503→
   504→    async def end_session(self, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
   505→        """End a session and save summary."""
   506→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   507→        if not session:
   508→            return None
   509→
   510→        session.is_active = False
   511→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   512→
   513→        # Generate session summary via Claude
   514→        try:
   515→            summary = await self._generate_summary(session)
   516→        except Exception as e:
   517→            print(f"[mira] Failed to generate summary for {user_id}: {e}")
   518→            summary = "Session ended before summary could be generated."
   519→
   520→        # Save to DB — wrapped in error handling so a DB failure
   521→        # doesn't prevent the session_ended event from reaching the frontend
   522→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   523→        try:
   524→            await save_session_summary(
   525→                db=db,
   526→                session_id=session.session_id,
   527→                summary=summary,
   528→                liked_items=session.liked_items,
   529→                reactions={
   530→                    "likes": session.likes,
   531→                    "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   532→                    "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   533→                },
   534→            )
   535→        except Exception as e:
   536→            print(f"[mira] Failed to save session summary: {e}")
   537→        finally:
   538→            await db.close()
   539→
   540→        # Emit session end to frontend
   541→        if self.sio:
   542→            await self.sio.emit(
   543→                "session_ended",
   544→                {
   545→                    "session_id": session.session_id,
   546→                    "summary": summary,
   547→                    "liked_items": session.liked_items,
   548→                    "stats": {
   549→                        "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   550→                        "likes": session.likes,
   551→                        "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   552→                    },
   553→                    "user_name": session.user_context.get("profile", {}).get("name", ""),
   554→                },
   555→                room=user_id,
   556→            )
   557→
   558→        del self.sessions[user_id]
   559→        return {"summary": summary, "liked_items": session.liked_items}
   560→
   561→    async def _generate_summary(self, session: SessionState) -> str:
   562→        """Ask Claude to generate a short session summary for memory."""
   563→        response = await self.client.messages.create(
   564→            model=HAIKU_MODEL,
   565→            max_tokens=200,
   566→            system="Summarize this styling session in 2-3 sentences for future reference. Include key style preferences discovered, items liked, and overall vibe.",
   567→            messages=[
   568→                {
   569→                    "role": "user",
   570→                    "content": f"Session had {session.items_shown} items shown, {session.likes} liked, {session.dislikes} disliked. Liked items: {json.dumps(session.liked_items[:5])}",
   571→                }
   572→            ],
   573→        )
   574→        return response.content[0].text
   575→
   576→    async def _graceful_shutdown(self, session: SessionState) -> None:
   577→        """Gracefully end a session when the API limit is reached.
   578→
   579→        Generates a warm closing speech, streams it via TTS, then ends the session.
   580→        """
   581→        user_id = session.user_id
   582→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   583→
   584→        # Generate and stream the closing speech so the user hears a goodbye
   585→        closing_speech = await self._generate_closing_speech(session)
   586→        await self._stream_text(user_id, closing_speech)
   587→        await self._stream_text(user_id, "", end_of_message=True)
   588→
   589→        # Brief pause to let TTS start playing before session teardown
   590→        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
   591→
   592→        # Guard: session may have been force-ended during the sleep
   593→        if user_id not in self.sessions:
   594→            print(f"[mira] Session already ended for {user_id} during graceful shutdown")
   595→            return
   596→
   597→        # End session — saves summary, emits session_ended with recap payload
   598→        await self.end_session(user_id)
   599→

600→    async def _generate_closing_speech(self, session: SessionState) -> str:
   601→        """Generate a warm, in-character closing message from Mira.
   602→
   603→        Separate from _generate_summary() because the summary is a clinical DB
   604→        record, while this is a spoken goodbye the user hears via TTS.
   605→        """
   606→        liked_names = [item.get("title", "an item") for item in session.liked_items[:5]]
   607→        liked_str = ", ".join(liked_names) if liked_names else "the styles we explored"
   608→
   609→        try:
   610→            response = await self.client.messages.create(
   611→                model=HAIKU_MODEL,
   612→                max_tokens=200,
   613→                system=(
   614→                    "You are Mira, a warm and stylish AI fashion advisor wrapping up a session. "
   615→                    "Give a brief closing recap (2-3 sentences). Mention their favorites, give a confidence boost, "
